title: pinus alba var. arctica
intro: Pinus alba var. arctica, also known as the Arctic White Pine, is a variety of the White Pine tree native to the Arctic regions of North America and Greenland. It is a type of evergreen coniferous tree that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reduce fever. The health benefits of Pinus alba var. arctica include its ability to improve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, and its potential to lower blood pressure. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
